target_solar_position.calculation: NOAA fractional-year equation-of-time and solar-declination approximation
target_solar_position.unreal_conversion: World +X is east and +Y is north. Incoming light rays use the reciprocal compass bearing (solar azimuth + 180 degrees); Unreal yaw from +X is 90 degrees minus that ray bearing, normalized to 96.0626 degrees.

source.provider: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
source.usage_note: Nearest routine station observation to the 11:50 a.m. target time; airport conditions are treated as regional weather evidence, not a claim of block-level microclimate.
